name: Local Account Enumeration via Net.exe
description: Detects use of net.exe or net1.exe to enumerate local user accounts and group memberships. Adversaries run 'net user' and 'net localgroup administrators' immediately after initial access to understand the account landscape prior to privilege escalation or lateral movement (T1087.001).
query: process where event.type == "start" and (process.name : ("net.exe", "net1.exe")) and process.command_line : ("* user*", "* localgroup*") and not process.command_line : ("*/add*", "*/delete*", "*/passwordchg*", "*/domain*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: WMI-Based Local Account Enumeration
description: Detects wmic.exe querying the Win32_UserAccount class or PowerShell Get-LocalUser/Get-LocalGroupMember cmdlets. WMI enumeration is favored by adversaries because it bypasses simple net.exe monitoring rules and still yields a full account inventory (T1087.001).
query:
process where event.type == "start" and (
  (process.name : "wmic.exe" and process.command_line : ("*useraccount*", "*Win32_UserAccount*", "*Win32_Group*")) or
  (process.name : ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and process.command_line : ("*Get-LocalUser*", "*Get-LocalGroupMember*", "*Get-LocalGroup*", "*Win32_UserAccount*"))
)
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Kerberos Service Ticket Request with RC4 Encryption (Kerberoasting)
description: Detects Windows Security Event 4769 where Kerberos service tickets (TGS-REQ) are requested with RC4 (etype 0x17/23) or DES encryption. This is the definitive network indicator of Kerberoasting attacks — adversaries request RC4-encrypted tickets for offline cracking against service account passwords (T1558.003).
query: event.code:"4769" and winlog.event_data.TicketEncryptionType:("0x17" or "0x18" or "0x3") and not winlog.event_data.ServiceName:(*$ or krbtgt)
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: AS-REP Roasting - Kerberos Pre-Authentication Not Required
description: Detects Windows Security Event 4768 (AS-REQ) where no pre-authentication data is present. Adversaries target accounts with DONT_REQUIRE_PREAUTH set to obtain AS-REP encrypted blobs for offline cracking without requiring domain credentials. Commonly executed via Rubeus asreproast or Impacket GetNPUsers (T1558.003).
query: event.code:"4768" and winlog.event_data.PreAuthType:("0" or "") and winlog.event_data.Status:("0x0" or "0x6") and not winlog.event_data.TargetUserName:(*$ or krbtgt)
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Account Enumeration Burst - Multi-Tool Sequence (Discovery Chain)
description: High-fidelity detection correlating multiple distinct enumeration tools (net.exe, whoami.exe, wmic.exe) executed in rapid sequence from the same host. Single-tool execution has moderate false positive rates; observing two or more distinct enumeration binaries within 2 minutes from the same process tree is strongly indicative of post-compromise reconnaissance (T1087.001, T1078.003).
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=2m
  [process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("net.exe", "net1.exe") and process.command_line : ("* user*", "* localgroup*")]
  [process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("whoami.exe", "wmic.exe") and process.command_line : ("*/all*", "*/priv*", "*useraccount*", "*Win32_UserAccount*")]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
